title: metallodrug_ribotac_cross_v1 — CROSS-AXIS J1 output rows
description: Output schema for _python_bridge/module/metallodrug_ribotac_cross.py. CROSS-AXIS integration J1: the METALLODRUG expansion-axis (Pt(II) coordination adduct chemistry — sequence-context-dependent, stoichiometric, kinetically-irreversible Pt-N7(guanine) coordinate bond) is run side-by-side with the RIBOTAC sub-axis (Disney-lab class, mass-action bifunctional recruitment of endogenous RNase-L for catalytic multi-turnover cleavage of a structured RNA target). Same OUTCOME class (RNA degradation), opposite MECHANISM class (metal-coordination chemistry ≠ enzyme recruitment). Both parent simulators are IMPORTED, never reimplemented (governance f3). The PASS sentinel certifies IN-SILICO simulator+metadata internal consistency ONLY — never a therapeutic, cytotoxic, antitumor, immunogenic, efficacy, regulatory or modality-superiority claim (AGENTS.tape g8/f2). Modalities are described by their own precedent (cisplatin/carboplatin/oxaliplatin for METALLODRUG; Disney-lab RIBOTACs for RIBOTAC) — never lattice-derived (g3/f1/f_lattice_fit). METALLODRUG is an expansion-axis (NOT core-5); RIBOTAC is a SUB-AXIS :> BIFUNCTIONAL (also NOT core-5). The cross does not introduce a new axis.

row_kind.description: Which mechanism pathway this row represents — the two pathway kinds are mechanism-disjoint and are NOT collapsed into a single rate.

target_name.description: Panel target identifier shared between the paired metallodrug and ribotac rows for the same RNA target.

paired_target_name.description: Echo of target_name used to assert metallodrug+ribotac rows are correctly paired per target.

rna_seq.description: RNA target sequence (A/C/G/U). Each panel target carries an r(GpG) motif so the Pt(II) coordination pathway is runnable on the same substrate the RIBOTAC pathway targets — making the side-by-side fair.

mechanism_class.description: Mechanism class. The cross asserts these are DISJOINT — same outcome class (RNA degradation), opposite mechanism class.

rate_type.description: Rate-type class. Pt(II) adduct chemistry is stoichiometric (one Pt-N7 coordinate-bond event neutralizes one site per RNA copy); RIBOTAC is catalytic-multi-turnover (N = k_cat · t_exposure RNA copies per molecule).

reversibility.description: Reversibility/turnover description for this mechanism (e.g. 'kinetically-irreversible-on-biological-timescale' for Pt(II) adducts; 'catalyst-released-after-cleavage (multi-turnover)' for RIBOTAC).

modality_stage.description: Development stage of the modality. Pt(II) family (cisplatin/carboplatin/oxaliplatin) is FDA-approved; RIBOTAC is research-stage (no approved RIBOTAC).

drug_precedent.description: Own modality precedent (g3/f1/f_lattice_fit — never lattice-derived). METALLODRUG: cisplatin/carboplatin/oxaliplatin (Pt(II) square-planar d8 coordinate-bond chemistry; Takahara 1995, Reedijk 2003, Hostetter et al. cisplatin-rRNA line 2009 onward). RIBOTAC: Disney-lab RNase-L-recruiting bifunctional class (Costales et al. JACS 2018, PNAS 2020).

rna_neutralized_per_drug_molecule.description: RNA copies neutralized per drug molecule under this mechanism. For METALLODRUG (stoichiometric) bounded above by 1.0 per Pt adduct event; for RIBOTAC equal to ternary_fraction · turnover_number (catalytic multi-turnover advantage).

real_limit_anchor_name.description: Name of the real-limit anchor for this mechanism (g1).

real_limit_citations.description: Non-empty citation list for the real-limit anchor(s) of this mechanism.

lattice_stance.description: Explicit statement that no n=6 lattice arithmetic is used in this row (g2 lattice-is-tool, f_lattice_fit).

in_silico_only.description: Always true — the row certifies in-silico simulator+metadata consistency only (g8/f2), never a therapeutic/clinical/regulatory claim.

metallodrug_metal.description: [metallodrug row only] Metal centre (Pt for the FDA Pt(II) family).

metallodrug_oxidation_state.description: [metallodrug row only] Oxidation state (+2 for Pt(II)).

metallodrug_d_count.description: [metallodrug row only] d-electron count (8 for Pt(II) square-planar).

metallodrug_geometry.description: [metallodrug row only] Coordination geometry (square-planar for Pt(II)).

metallodrug_indication.description: [metallodrug row only] Approved oncology indication of the precedent drug.

pt_n7_bond_angstrom_anchor.description: [metallodrug row only] Real-limit anchor: cisplatin Pt-N7(guanine) coordinate-bond length (~2.0 Å, Takahara 1995).

pt_n7_bond_angstrom_tolerance.description: [metallodrug row only] Acceptance tolerance for the recomputed Pt-N7 distance vs the anchor.

pt_n7_recomputed_angstrom.description: [metallodrug row only] Recomputed Pt-N radial distance from the parent module's square-planar geometry.

pt_n7_anchor_match.description: [metallodrug row only] True iff the recomputed Pt-N distance is within tolerance of the ~2.0 Å Takahara 1995 real-limit anchor.

square_planar_d8_cfse_delta_oct.description: [metallodrug row only] Closed-form d8 square-planar CFSE in units of Delta_oct (Griffith & Orgel 1957 ligand-field theory; computed by the parent metallodrug sim).

rGpG_site_count.description: [metallodrug row only] Count of overlapping r(GpG) coordination sites on the RNA target — Pt(II) prefers the 1,2-intrastrand d/r-GpG site (Takahara 1995; Reedijk 2003).

rGpG_site_density.description: [metallodrug row only] r(GpG) site density = sites / (n−1).

sequence_context_dependent.description: [metallodrug row only] True — Pt(II) adduct formation is sequence-context-dependent (d/r-GpG preference).

ribotac_panel_name.description: [ribotac row only] Identifier of the parent ribotac_sim.RIBOTAC_PANEL parameter set parameterising this row.

dot_bracket.description: [ribotac row only] Vienna dot-bracket secondary structure of the target RNA (parent RIBOZYME-axis Nussinov solver, imported via ribotac_sim — no fork at any remove).

num_base_pairs.description: [ribotac row only] Number of base pairs in the dot-bracket structure.

pair_density.description: [ribotac row only] 2 · num_base_pairs / rna_length_nt — proxy for target structuredness.

dot_bracket_balanced.description: [ribotac row only] Dot-bracket balance check (parent solver).

rna_structured.description: [ribotac row only] True iff pair_density > 0 (structured target — RIBOTACs need a structured RNA).

k_d1_nM.description: [ribotac row only] Dissociation constant of the RNA-binding warhead to the target (nM).

k_d2_nM.description: [ribotac row only] Intrinsic dissociation constant of the recruiter warhead to RNase-L (nM).

alpha.description: [ribotac row only] Linker-tethered ternary cooperativity factor (≥ 1).

k_d2_effective_nM.description: [ribotac row only] Effective second-site K_d = K_d2 / alpha (nM).

binary_fraction.description: [ribotac row only] Mass-action occupancy of the binary Target·R complex (Guldberg & Waage 1864 real-limit; bounded by 1.0).

rnase_site_fraction.description: [ribotac row only] Mass-action occupancy of the RNase-L recruiter site given the binary complex.

ternary_fraction.description: [ribotac row only] Ternary Target·R·RNase-L fraction = binary_fraction · rnase_site_fraction.

k_cat_per_s.description: [ribotac row only] Recruited-RNase-L catalytic turnover rate for the bound RNA (1/s).

cycle_time_s.description: [ribotac row only] Catalytic cycle time tau = 1 / k_cat (s).

t_exposure_s.description: [ribotac row only] Exposure window over which turnovers accumulate (s).

turnover_number.description: [ribotac row only] Catalytic turnover number N = k_cat · t_exposure. Real-limit: a genuine catalyst has N > 1 (Cornish-Bowden 2012).

stoichiometric_turnover.description: [ribotac row only] Stoichiometric reference turnover number (= 1 by definition).

is_catalytic.description: [ribotac row only] True iff turnover_number > 1 — the defining RIBOTAC catalytic property.
